subheading: Relight, refine, and polish a portrait into a professional headshot with open, editable ComfyUI workflows you control end to end.
extendedDescription:
The most common complaint about an AI headshot generator is that the result looks like someone else. You upload your photos, wait, and get back a polished stranger with your vague resemblance. That happens because most tools train a fresh model on your uploads and then generate brand-new images from scratch. The ComfyUI workflows on this page work the other way around: they start from a real photo of you and improve it, so the person in the final headshot is unmistakably you.

Three jobs turn an ordinary portrait into a professional one, and these workflows do each as a step you control. A character portrait relighting workflow built on Nano Banana Pro re-lights a flat phone snapshot with soft, even studio light, which is the single biggest difference between a snapshot and a studio headshot. A skin enhancer and upscaler combining Z-Image-Turbo and SDXL sharpens facial detail while keeping pores and texture, so the corporate headshot avoids the waxy, over-smoothed look that gives AI portraits away. Qwen-Image-Edit light migration can instead borrow the lighting from a reference photo you like and apply it to yours, and a Magnific skin enhancer cleans up the final frame.

Knowing what each step does helps you get a better result. Relighting changes how your face is lit: it softens harsh shadows, balances the light across your face, and sets a professional mood. Enhancement and upscaling then work on resolution and texture, recovering detail in the eyes and skin and enlarging the frame without the smearing a basic resize produces. Most professional headshots benefit from a relight followed by an enhancement pass, run in sequence, and these workflows let you do exactly that and stop when it looks right.

Because every step is open, you set the lighting direction, decide how far to push detail, and re-run any pass instead of accepting a single fixed output. There is no watermark and no hidden model deciding how your face should look. A professional photoshoot often locks you into a single look per session; here you can iterate as many times as you like on Comfy Cloud or locally in ComfyUI.

These workflows refine a real photo of you, so they are ideal when you have a usable portrait or selfie that needs studio polish, rather than when you want a model to invent brand-new poses. If your starting photo is sharp, front-facing, and evenly lit, a relight followed by an enhancement pass is usually all it takes to reach a result you would be happy to put on LinkedIn.

styles.title: Studio relighting
styles.description: Re-light a flat phone photo with soft, even studio light using the Nano Banana Pro portrait relighting workflow.

styles.title: Lighting migration
styles.description: Borrow the lighting setup from a reference image and apply it to your portrait with Qwen-Image-Edit.

styles.title: Skin and detail enhancement
styles.description: Sharpen facial detail and clean up skin with Z-Image-Turbo, SDXL, and Magnific while keeping natural texture.

styles.title: Resolution upscaling
styles.description: Take the finished headshot to a crisp, full-resolution file suitable for print, badges, and large displays.

styles.title: Stylized variations
styles.description: Turn a selfie into stylized portrait variations when you want something playful rather than a straight headshot.

howToUse: Pick the workflow that matches your goal: relighting, skin enhancement, or upscaling.
Open it on Comfy Cloud with no install, or load it in your local ComfyUI.
Upload a clear, front-facing photo with your face unobstructed.
Set the lighting direction and how strongly to enhance skin and detail, starting conservative.
Run the workflow and compare the result against your original to judge the change.
Upscale the version you like for a print-ready file, then download it at full resolution.

faqItems.question: How does an AI headshot generator work in ComfyUI?
faqItems.answer: Instead of training a model on many photos, these workflows take one portrait you already have and improve it. They relight it, enhance skin and detail, and upscale the result, with each step exposed so you can adjust it.

faqItems.question: Are AI headshots good enough for LinkedIn?
faqItems.answer: Yes, when they start from a real photo of you. Because these workflows preserve natural skin texture and let you control the lighting, the output reads as a studio headshot rather than an obvious AI render, which suits LinkedIn and corporate directories.

faqItems.question: Can I run these AI headshot generator workflows myself?
faqItems.answer: Yes. The workflows are open and editable. You can run them locally in ComfyUI, or on Comfy Cloud with no setup, and there are no watermarks on the output.

faqItems.question: What photo should I upload for the best professional headshot?
faqItems.answer: Use a clear, front-facing photo with even lighting and your face unobstructed. A higher-resolution input gives the skin enhancement and upscaling steps more real detail to work with.

faqItems.question: Will the headshot still look like me?
faqItems.answer: These workflows refine your actual photo rather than inventing a new face, so your features stay intact. You also control how far the skin and detail enhancement pushes, which keeps the result faithful to your real appearance. If a pass starts to look too smooth, lower the enhancement strength and run it again until it matches how you actually look.

faqItems.question: Can I make a headshot from a casual selfie?
faqItems.answer: Yes. The relighting and skin enhancement workflows are designed to turn a casual, well-lit selfie into a more polished portrait. The cleaner and sharper your selfie, the stronger the final headshot from a selfie will be. Front-facing shots with your whole face visible and no heavy backlight give the relighting step the most to work with.

faqItems.question: Why do AI headshots often look fake, and how do these avoid it?
faqItems.answer: Most generators invent a new image and over-smooth the skin, which lands in the uncanny valley. These workflows keep your real photo and let you control how strong the enhancement is, so you can dial back any pass that looks too plastic and preserve natural skin texture.

faqItems.question: How does this compare to booking a photographer?
faqItems.answer: A professional headshot session usually locks you into a single look per sitting. These workflows run in local ComfyUI or on Comfy Cloud with no setup, so you can refine the lighting and detail and re-export as many versions as you want.

faqItems.question: How is this different from a one-click headshot app?
faqItems.answer: One-click apps return a fixed result from a hidden model and often charge per photo. These are open workflows where you control relighting, detail, and upscaling, re-run any step, and keep full-resolution downloads with no watermark.

faqItems.question: Do I need to install anything to run these portrait workflows?
faqItems.answer: No. Comfy Cloud opens the workflow in your browser with nothing to install, so you can relight and enhance a portrait on a laptop without a GPU. If you prefer to run it on your own machine, the same workflow loads in local ComfyUI.

faqItems.question: What hardware do I need to run the skin enhancement locally?
faqItems.answer: The Z-Image-Turbo skin and detail pass is a distilled 6B model that runs in about 8 steps; an FP8 build fits in roughly 8GB of VRAM and community GGUF quants squeeze onto 6GB cards, while the full BF16 build wants 14 to 16GB. If your card is smaller, run the same pass on Comfy Cloud instead and let it handle the GPU.

faqItems.question: Does this cost anything to run on Comfy Cloud?
faqItems.answer: Comfy Cloud is a paid plan that starts at $20 per month for the Standard tier, with Creator and Pro tiers above it, and it bills for active GPU time while a workflow runs. Running the same workflows locally in ComfyUI uses your own GPU with no per-image charge.
